title: goodenia quadrilocularis
intro: Goodenia quadrilocularis, also known as the four-angled goodenia, is a native Australian plant species that belongs to the Goodeniaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Western Australia for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to be effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial infections, and promoting wound healing. Goodenia quadrilocularis can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
